commands.review_summary:
The branch adds top-level and account-scoped Signal target aliases, schema/types/docs, directory exposure, and alias resolution across Signal outbound, default target, approval, and session-route paths.

PR surface: Source +383, Tests +683, Docs +55, Generated 0. Total +1121 across 19 files.

Reproducibility: not applicable. this is a feature PR rather than a reported broken existing behavior. Current main does not expose `channels.signal.aliases`, and the PR body/tests define the intended new behavior.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Maintainer-labeled draft feature PR needs product/config acceptance and conflict resolution, not autonomous cleanup closure or repair.

target.title: feat(signal): add target aliases
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Signal delivery targets are hard to reuse because DMs can be phone numbers or UUIDs, while groups use opaque group IDs. This adds OpenClaw-side Signal aliases so users can configure friendly names like `signal:me` or `signal:ops` once and use them in normal Signal delivery paths.

## Why This Change Was Made

The alias mapping belongs in the Signal plugin boundary because it is Signal-specific target normalization, not a cross-channel address book or Signal contact mutation. The implementation resolves config-backed aliases before outbound delivery, default target lookup, approval authorization/routing, directory listing, and outbound session route construction so Signal history and approval reactions use canonical Signal targets.

This intentionally does not mutate Signal contacts, discover live Signal contacts, or add wildcard/broad Signal access behavior.

## User Impact

Users can configure `channels.signal.aliases` or `channels.signal.accounts.<id>.aliases` with DM and group targets, then send to aliases such as `signal:ops`. Per-account aliases inherit top-level aliases and may override them. Invalid, blank, or recursive aliases fail before delivery with a clear Signal alias error.

The Signal directory surface can list configured aliases as peers or groups, and exact alias lookups keep peer and group targets separated.

## Evidence

Manual Signal proof from Jesse:

- Live gateway was running from worktree/branch `openclaw-signal-aliases-directory` / `jesse/signal-aliases-directory`.
- Temp config mapped `channels.signal.aliases.ops` to `group:l+j9mBRLUCHN+2xue4rlVLOiEbVsKi7yF3WacQazOB0=` and `channels.signal.aliases.me` to Jesse's configured E.164 alias, with `channels.signal.defaultTo = "signal:me"`.
- Safe ingress stayed narrow: DMs disabled, group policy allowlist, Testing realm IDs only.
- `openclaw message send --channel signal --target signal:ops --message "alias ops smoke ..." --json` resolved to `group:l+j9mBRLUCHN+2xue4rlVLOiEbVsKi7yF3WacQazOB0=`, and Jesse confirmed it appeared in the Testing realm.
- From the Testing realm, Jesse asked the agent to message him via `signal:me`; Jesse confirmed the response was visible in the intended Signal DM.

Automated checks on the pushed PR-head content:

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/signal/src/aliases.test.ts extensions/signal/src/core.test.ts extensions/signal/src/approval-auth.test.ts extensions/signal/src/approval-handler.runtime.test.ts extensions/signal/src/approval-reactions.test.ts src/infra/outbound/targets.test.ts` passed: 2 Vitest shards, 155 tests.
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 extensions/signal/src/aliases.test.ts extensions/signal/src/aliases.ts extensions/signal/src/approval-auth.test.ts extensions/signal/src/approval-auth.ts extensions/signal/src/approval-handler.runtime.test.ts extensions/signal/src/approval-handler.runtime.ts extensions/signal/src/approval-reactions.test.ts extensions/signal/src/approval-reactions.ts extensions/signal/src/channel.ts extensions/signal/src/core.test.ts extensions/signal/src/shared.ts src/infra/outbound/targets.test.ts` passed.
- `git diff --check` passed.
- Autoreview passed clean on `jesse/signal-aliases-pr-closeout` against `origin/main` after fixing two accepted findings: alias-configured approval reactions now compare canonical Signal targets, and Signal outbound text sanitization is restored.

Known proof gap:

- Testbox-through-Crabbox `corepack pnpm check:changed` was attempted on `tbx_01kwcff7je54x3vy5n2xmswerw` with provider `blacksmith-testbox`, but it did not produce a usable pass. The delegated run escalated to broad/all-lane sync and exited `255` as lint started, without an actionable lint finding in the captured output; the wrapper also reported a runner portal sync `401` after cleanup. Focused local proof above is current for the touched Signal/outbound surfaces.
- No new Signal alias-resolution log/metadata field was added in this closeout. The shared outbound target resolution contract currently returns only the canonical `to`, so this PR relies on `message send --json` resolved target evidence and manual confirmation; gateway logs still do not literally show `signal:me` resolving to Jesse's configured E.164 alias.
target.author: jesse-merhi
